Lake Powell SXS Extravaganza
Oct 10th – Oct 12th
- Time4:00 PM to 5:00 PM
Welcome to the Lake Powell SXS Extravaganza!
Get ready for a heart-pounding adventure set against the stunning backdrop of Big Water, Utah! The Lake Powell SXS Extravaganza invites you to explore two spectacular UTV loops, each offering a unique and unforgettable experience.
Choose your adventure on any day of the event:
Lake Powell Overlooks Loop: Marvel at the expansive beauty of Lake Powell as you ride along scenic trails with panoramic views of the shimmering waters and rugged red rock canyons. This loop promises breathtaking vistas and unforgettable photo ops.
Grosvenor Arch Loop: Discover the awe-inspiring Grosvenor Arch, a majestic double arch formation that stands as a testament to nature’s grandeur. Traverse through striking desert landscapes and be captivated by this geological marvel.
Base Camp for the event will be the Rodeway Inn in Big Water, UT. Secure your stay at the Inn or bring your RV and park in their parking lot for a small fee. Comfort and convenience are just steps away from all the action!
Kick off each day with a hearty breakfast to energize your adventure. After a day on the trails, join us for a delicious dinner on Saturday night, followed by an exciting raffle and a thrilling Quick Draw Poker game. The poker winner will take home cash prizes, and all proceeds will benefit a local charity.
Whether you’re here for the epic rides, the spectacular scenery, or the chance to give back, the Lake Powell SXS Extravaganza offers an experience like no other. Register now, book your room or RV spot, and choose your adventure!

Font size has a huge impact on user experience on any operating system platform, and Windows 11 is no exception. To enlarge or reduce the displayed text size on Windows 11, you just need to follow these simple steps.
1. First, press the Windows + i key combination to open the Windows Settings application. Or u can also right-click the Start button on the taskbar and select Settings in the pop-up menu.
2. When the Settings interface opens, click Accessibility in the list on the left, then click Text Size on the right screen.
3. In the text size settings, look for the slider labeled Text Size. Then, with the mouse pointer click and drag the circle on the slider to the right or left to adjust the size of the system font.
4. As you drag the slider, you'll see a percentage number appear directly above it, indicating the ratio of the font size you're adjusting, to the original standard size of 100%. So at the 200% size, the system font you choose will be twice as large as the default. Once you've chosen the right size, click Apply.
5. All open windows will instantly refresh visually and you will see the change in text size applied immediately. Note that choosing a font size that is too large can lead to an unreasonable look. You'll sometimes see ellipses (three dots) when the displayed text is too large for the space provided for them.
Now close Settings and the new text size has been applied and maintained, even after you restart your computer.
Wish you have a good experience using Windows 11

Cross-modality Data Augmentation for End-to-End Sign Language Translation
End-to-end sign language translation (SLT) aims to convert sign language videos into spoken language texts directly without intermediate representations. It has been a challenging task due to the modality gap between sign videos and texts and the data scarcity of labeled data. To tackle these challenges, we propose a novel Cross-modality Data Augmentation (XmDA) framework to transfer the powerful gloss-to-text translation capabilities to end-to-end sign language translation (i.e. video-to-text) by exploiting pseudo gloss-text pairs from the sign gloss translation model. Specifically, XmDA consists of two key components, namely, cross-modality mix-up and cross-modality knowledge distillation. The former explicitly encourages the alignment between sign video features and gloss embeddings to bridge the modality gap. The latter utilizes the generation knowledge from gloss-to-text teacher models to guide the spoken language text generation. Experimental results on two widely used SLT datasets, i.e., PHOENIX-2014T and CSL-Daily, demonstrate that the proposed XmDA framework significantly and consistently outperforms the baseline models. Extensive analyses confirm our claim that XmDA enhances spoken language text generation by reducing the representation distance between videos and texts, as well as improving the processing of low-frequency words and long sentences.

Have a Blast is a co-op multiplayer party game coming to PC and Nintendo Switch on August 26th!
Breda, Netherlands, July 21, 2021 – More than four years ago Bojan Endrovski set out to create the most chaotic competitive space brawler possible, without compromising the depth and skill required to provide a challenge for gamers.
Bojan has been making couch co-op and PVP games like the ballet dancing Bounden and sensual twister Fingle for over a decade. The team at Firenut Games is proud to present the fruits of their labours, Have a Blast is firing its way onto the Nintendo Switch and Steam on August 26, 2021.
Have a Blast is a high-energy, kinetic, multiplayer party game. Blast through the most ridiculously hazardous corners of the galaxy using your surroundings to your advantage. Outmaneuver your friends and shatter your enemies into colorful little bits.
Battle your way through the galaxy in fifteen unique arenas, with new locations coming in the future. Navigate your way through old classics like asteroids, icy comets, mythical monsters, and derelict machinery.
All the ships in Have a Blast are small and nimble, enabling you to navigate the chaos of each level. There are five ships for you to choose from, each has its own powerful ability, each requiring a slightly different approach to the competition. Beware, even the strongest attacks can be countered, remember, it’s never too late to turn the tide of battle.
- Deathmatch – The last pilot standing wins the round in this familiar mode. The first to win five rounds wins the match.
- Mothership – Keep your mothership safe and it will provide you with health, ammo, and some refuge. Lose your mothership and you’ll be sleeping with the space fishes.
- Team match – Befriend your enemies and ruin friendships in this team-based competition. Any combination of players is possible in a team vs team play.
- Challenges – Demonstrate your mastery of space by completing specific challenges against AI components.
- 15 arenas to enjoy with your Friends
- 5 ships with unique specs
- Variety of game modes: Deathmatch, Mothership, Team match, Challenges
- Up to 4 players in local mode
Available August 26, 2021, on Nintendo Switch and Steam. In addition, for all those who pre-order, their game before launch will get a 20% discount on Nintendo Switch.

In 3D printing, the success of a print often hinges on a small but critical factor: first-layer adhesion. If the filament doesn’t stick to the build plate properly during the initial layers, the entire print is at risk of failure. To address this, many users turn to adhesion aids like glue sticks, hairspray, and adhesive sheets. These simple additions can drastically improve bed adhesion, particularly for challenging materials or specific surface types.
This guide explores how glue sticks, hairspray, and adhesive sheets work, their pros and cons, the best use cases for each, and proper application techniques. By understanding these tools, you’ll be better equipped to prevent warping, improve print consistency, and extend your printer’s reliability.
Why Use Adhesion Aids in 3D Printing?
While many modern printers include textured or coated beds that offer decent adhesion out of the box, not all filaments behave the same way. Certain materials—such as ABS, PETG, and flexible filaments—are more likely to warp or detach during printing. Surface wear, temperature fluctuations, or environmental factors like humidity can also affect adhesion.
Adhesion aids help bridge the gap between filament and surface. Whether through chemical bonding or physical texture, they create a stronger grip between the first layer and the bed, reducing print failures and edge lifting.
Overview of Common Adhesion Aids
1. Glue Sticks
Glue sticks are perhaps the most popular and widely used bed adhesion aid. Most commonly, users apply a thin layer of PVA-based (polyvinyl acetate) glue—similar to the standard school glue—onto the build surface. This glue provides a sticky layer when warm and releases cleanly after cooling.
Benefits of Glue Sticks:
- Easy to apply and remove
- Non-toxic and safe to use indoors
- Effective for multiple filament types (PLA, ABS, PETG, TPU)
- Helps protect the build surface from scratches or damage
- Requires cleaning after several prints
- Uneven application can cause ridges or bumps
- Can degrade print surface clarity or gloss
- Use a fresh glue stick and apply a thin, even layer to the center or full bed.
- Apply while the bed is warm for smoother spreading.
- Wipe clean with warm water every few prints to prevent buildup.
Hairspray is another surprisingly effective adhesion promoter, particularly for glass beds. The active ingredient—typically polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP) or similar polymers—creates a slightly tacky surface when sprayed. Hairspray works well with heated glass beds and offers a glossy finish when the print is removed cleanly.
Benefits of Hairspray:
- Quick and convenient to apply
- Covers large areas in seconds
- Provides consistent adhesion for PLA and ABS
- Allows for smooth, glossy bottom layers on glass
- Can leave residue on surrounding printer parts
- Not ideal for enclosed spaces due to aerosol inhalation risks
- Requires cleaning with alcohol or soap to avoid buildup
- Use unscented, aerosol hairspray with PVP as the main ingredient.
- Spray onto a cool bed and allow to dry before heating.
- Use in a well-ventilated area to avoid inhaling fumes.
3. Adhesive Sheets
Adhesive sheets are physical surfaces that stick to your build plate to improve adhesion. These include PEI sheets, BuildTak, Garolite, and other branded adhesion films. Unlike glue or spray, these are semi-permanent solutions and are ideal for users who want consistency across multiple prints.
Benefits of Adhesive Sheets:
- Reliable long-term adhesion
- Excellent durability with correct use
- No daily prep required once installed
- Available for specific materials like nylon (Garolite) or ABS (BuildTak)
- Initial installation must be precise to avoid air bubbles
- Over time, certain sheets may peel or lose effectiveness
- May require extra care when removing prints to avoid surface damage
- Clean the sheet regularly with IPA (isopropyl alcohol).
- Use a flexible steel sheet beneath for easier removal (if compatible).
- Replace sheets when adhesion fails or visible wear appears.
Choosing the Right Adhesion Aid for Your Needs
The best adhesion aid depends on your printing setup, filament type, and print environment. Below is a quick breakdown to help guide your decision:
Adhesion Aid | Best For | Not Ideal For | Key Features |
Glue Stick | PETG, TPU, ABS | Very smooth beds without texture | Washable, good release after cooling |
Hairspray | PLA, ABS on glass | Enclosed printers with poor airflow | Fast application, glossy finish |
Adhesive Sheets | Nylon, ABS, PETG | Users who change beds often | Long-term adhesion, durability |
If you’re printing with a variety of materials, keeping multiple adhesion options on hand gives you flexibility and better results across jobs.
How to Apply Each Adhesion Aid Effectively
Proper application ensures you get the best results from your adhesion method. Poor application often causes more problems than using no aid at all.
Applying Glue Stick:
- Heat the bed to 50–60°C for smoother application.
- Apply a thin, even layer directly to the build plate.
- Spread excess with a damp cloth or sponge if needed.
- After the print, clean the bed every few uses to remove dried residue.
- Shake the can and hold it about 20–30 cm from the bed.
- Spray a light mist evenly across the build surface.
- Let the surface dry completely before heating.
- Clean the bed weekly with alcohol to prevent film buildup.
Installing Adhesive Sheets:
- Thoroughly clean and dry the build surface.
- Align one edge and peel back the liner while applying gradually.
- Use a flat scraper or credit card to press out air bubbles.
- Trim excess with a hobby knife and clean gently with IPA between prints.
When You Should Avoid Adhesion Aids
In some situations, adhesion aids are unnecessary or may cause more harm than good:
- With textured PEI surfaces, adhesion is often already strong enough.
- For very small prints, added stickiness can make part removal difficult.
- Overuse of glue or spray can result in poor surface finish or nozzle clogging due to accumulated debris.
- If you’re using flexible build plates, most prints will release cleanly without additional products.
Always test a small print first before applying adhesives across the entire bed.
Maintenance Tips and Safety Considerations
To ensure your bed stays clean, safe, and effective with adhesion aids:
- Avoid inhaling aerosol products like hairspray in enclosed spaces.
- Keep glue sticks capped and dry to maintain consistency.
- Never apply adhesives directly onto a hot nozzle or a powered printer.
- Always clean off old layers of glue or spray to prevent buildup.
- Keep a dedicated cloth or sponge for wiping glue to avoid contamination.
Proper maintenance ensures your prints stay clean, your build plate lasts longer, and your printer performs reliably over time.
Glue sticks, hairspray, and adhesive sheets are all valuable tools in the 3D printing toolkit, especially when dealing with materials or models that challenge bed adhesion. Each has its unique strengths: glue sticks for versatility, hairspray for ease of use, and adhesive sheets for long-term consistency.
Choosing the right solution depends on your material, surface type, and workflow. With correct application and maintenance, these adhesion aids can significantly reduce failed prints, save time, and enhance the quality of your 3D printing experience.
While new bed materials and automatic leveling technologies continue to evolve, mastering the basics of adhesion aids remains one of the most effective ways to ensure print success.
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
Is it safe to use hairspray on a heated bed?
Yes, as long as it is applied while the bed is cool and used in a well-ventilated area. Allow the spray to dry before heating.
Can I mix adhesion aids, like using glue and a PEI sheet?
It’s not recommended. Using glue on PEI can damage the coating over time and reduce its natural adhesion properties.
How often should I clean the glue or hairspray off my bed?
Every 3–5 prints is a good general rule. Clean more often if you notice streaking, cloudiness, or inconsistent adhesion.
Do adhesive sheets wear out over time?
Yes. Depending on usage, they may lose stickiness or develop wear marks. Most sheets last for dozens to hundreds of prints with proper care.
Which adhesion aid is best for flexible filaments?
Glue sticks are ideal for flexible filaments like TPU. They provide grip without excessive stickiness, making part removal easier.

Buyer Guide 3D Printing Supplies
It’s always a good idea to have a firm understanding of each individual component, and go from there.- Filament, or “muck.” The filament is a plastic used in 3D printing. It’s usually available in different diameters and colors.
- Printer. There are different kinds of 3D printers, including desktop, industrial, and open-source models.
- Software. There are several different types of printing software, including slicers, slicer-creator, and hosts.
- Support Material. Support material is a support material used in 3D printing. There are usually 2 types: soluble support material and solid support material
- PLA (Polylactic acid): PLA filament is biodegradable, renewable, and compostable.
- ABS (Acrylonitrile butadiene styrene): ABS filament is stronger, tougher, and better than PLA.
How to choose 3D Printing Supplies
If you are interested in learning more about 3D printing, you may have already started shopping for the materials and tools you’ll need to create the objects you want.- 3D printing is the process by which a three-dimensional object is created one layer at a time. There are six different types of 3D printing materials to choose from:
- The filament: This is the material, such as plastic, metal, or ceramics, that is fed into a 3D printer.
- Thermal resistance: This refers to how resistant a material is to heat. Some materials, such as PLA, are more thermally sensitive.
- The layer thickness: The layer thickness of the material determines how dense the surface can be. The higher the number, the thicker the layer.
- The curing speed: This refers to how long it takes for a material to solidify.
- The bonding strength: This is the ability of the material to stick to itself or to another object.
Stability: This refers to how resistant a material is to warping or shrinking. Some materials, such as ABS, are more elastic.
When choosing your materials, it’s important to consider which items you plan to print. The printing process requires certain materials like resin, which can be toxic and flammable.
In addition to the materials, you will also want to consider the tools you will need. A 3D printer itself can cost several thousand dollars, so it’s important to choose wisely.
- The size of the printer: When choosing a printer, it’s important to consider the size of the object you plan to print.
- The printing speed: Depending on your needs, you may need a faster or slower printer.
- The resolution: The resolution refers to the level of detail you can include in your print.
- The printing volume: The bigger the printer, the more material it can process at a time.
- The filament capacity: If you plan to print bigger objects, you may need a bigger capacity.
How to maintain 3D Printing Supplies
3D printing is a new technology that is changing the way people create and engineer new products.- To clean the filament, use soap and water to wet your filament, then wipe it dry with a paper towel.
- To clean the nozzles, wipe them with a damp rag.
- To clean the build plate, wipe it with a clean, lint-free cloth.
- To clean the extruder, use compressed air.
- To clean the build plate, first, remove any building material or part from the plate. Then, use a paper towel or paper towel moistened with rubbing alcohol to wipe off the build plate.
- To clean the extruder, first, unplug the extruder from the 3D printer. Then, moisten a paper towel or paper towel with rubbing alcohol and wipe down the extruder.
- To clean the 3D printer, use compressed air to blow loose dust out of the printer.
- To clean the filament, use compressed air to blow loose dust out of the filament.
- To clean the nozzle, use compressed air to blow loose dust out of the nozzle.
- To clean the build plate, use compressed air to blow loose dust out of the build plate.
- To clean the extruder, use compressed air to blow loose dust out of the extruder.
- To clean the filament, unplug the extruder from the 3D printer and use compressed air to blow loose dust out of the filament.
- To clean the nozzle, unplug the nozzle from the 3D printer and use compressed air to blow loose dust out of the nozzle.

If you’re looking for a sustainable and adorable craft idea, this Crochet Cactus Keychain Made with Pet Bottle Cap – TUTORIAL is the perfect project. This creative DIY combines crochet skills with upcycling, turning a plastic bottle cap into a charming little cactus keychain.
By following this Crochet Cactus Keychain Made with Pet Bottle Cap – TUTORIAL, you’ll not only create a fun accessory but also help reduce plastic waste in an artistic way. It’s a fantastic project for crafters of all levels and a unique gift idea for plant lovers and eco-conscious friends.
In this step-by-step guide, you’ll learn how to use a PET bottle cap as the base for a crocheted cactus, making it sturdy, stylish, and environmentally friendly. Let’s bring together creativity, sustainability, and crochet in one vibrant keychain!
1. Materials Needed for Your Crochet Cactus Keychain
Before starting this Crochet Cactus Keychain Made with Pet Bottle Cap – TUTORIAL, gather all your supplies so you can enjoy a smooth, uninterrupted crafting session.
You’ll need a PET bottle cap—preferably a flat, standard-sized one. Clean and dry it well before use. This cap will form the base of your cactus pot.
Use worsted weight yarn in green for the cactus and brown or terracotta for the pot. You may also want white or pink yarn for decorative flowers on the top.
You’ll need a crochet hook suited to your yarn—typically a 2.5 mm to 3.5 mm hook is ideal for small amigurumi-style pieces.
Other essentials include a darning needle, scissors, polyester stuffing or scrap yarn for filling, and a keyring or lobster clasp for attaching to your keys or bag.
Optional but fun additions include tiny beads, embroidery thread for eyes or smiley faces, and glue for reinforcing attachments like the keyring.
Having everything at your fingertips will make the crafting process fun, efficient, and creative from the first stitch to the last.
2. Crocheting the Pot Around the Bottle Cap
The first part of the Crochet Cactus Keychain Made with Pet Bottle Cap – TUTORIAL focuses on turning the PET cap into a sturdy, yarn-wrapped flowerpot.
Start by creating a magic ring and working in rounds with your brown or terracotta yarn. Increase gradually to form a flat circle that matches the diameter of the bottle cap.
Once you’ve reached the right width, continue working without increases to build the sides of the pot. This forms a “sleeve” that will fit snugly around the cap.
Slide the PET bottle cap into the base and continue crocheting over it. You can add a dab of glue underneath for extra hold, but a tight crochet sleeve will typically secure it well.
Continue crocheting upwards for two or three more rounds to form the rim of the pot. You can use front-loop-only stitches to define the edge for a decorative touch.
Finish off and weave in the ends. Now you have a reusable plastic cap completely transformed into a handmade cactus pot—adorable and eco-friendly.
3. Making the Cactus and Assembling the Keychain
Now it’s time to create the cactus part of your Crochet Cactus Keychain Made with Pet Bottle Cap – TUTORIAL. This step brings the greenery and charm to the project.
Using green yarn, start with a magic ring and crochet in the round to form a small, slightly oval shape. This will represent the cactus body.
After a few increase rounds, maintain the stitch count to build height, then decrease and stuff the piece with fiberfill. Keep it firm so the cactus stands upright.
You can add decorative ridges using front-post stitches or by shaping the body with light stuffing and tight stitches. Play with texture for a realistic look.
Sew the finished cactus onto the top of the pot base. Make sure it’s centered and tightly attached, especially if you plan to use the keychain regularly.
Next, attach the keyring or clasp. You can sew it directly onto the top of the cactus or create a small chain loop to hang it from the side.
Your cactus keychain is almost ready—now it’s time to add those final touches that make it extra special and personal.
4. Personalizing and Finishing Your Crochet Cactus Keychain
This section of the Crochet Cactus Keychain Made with Pet Bottle Cap – TUTORIAL is all about adding character and details that make your keychain unique.
Crochet a small flower using white, pink, or yellow yarn and sew it onto the cactus. A simple five-petal flower can be made with a magic ring and basic stitches.
You can add a tiny face using embroidery thread—just two eyes and a smile go a long way in creating a cute and friendly cactus expression.
If you want a more playful look, use safety eyes and a small felt heart as a decoration. These elements make the keychain perfect for gifting.
For extra stability, reinforce the keyring area with a few stitches and consider using a small drop of glue. This ensures long-lasting use.
Add beads or charms if you want to decorate the keyring itself. Personal messages or initials can make your keychain even more meaningful.
With all these final touches, your crochet cactus keychain transforms into a complete, charming project you’ll be proud to carry or give away.
FAQ – Crochet Cactus Keychain Made with Pet Bottle Cap
Is this project beginner-friendly?
Yes! The stitches used are simple (magic ring, single crochet, increases and decreases). It’s a great first project for anyone learning amigurumi or keychain crafting.
What type of bottle cap should I use?
Use any standard PET bottle cap, like those from soda or water bottles. Just make sure it’s clean, dry, and undamaged for the best results.
Can I use different yarn types?
Yes. While worsted-weight acrylic yarn is best for structure, you can also try cotton for a firmer texture or lighter yarns for a smaller keychain version.
How do I attach the keyring securely?
You can sew a small yarn loop onto the top of the cactus or use a jump ring to clip it into a loop of stitches. Reinforce with glue if needed.
What size should the cactus be?
Finished size varies depending on yarn and hook size, but typically the keychain is 2 to 3 inches tall—small enough to fit in your hand or pocket.
Can I wash the keychain?
Spot cleaning is recommended. Handwash gently and avoid soaking the PET cap, as water can affect the structure if not dried properly.
The Crochet Cactus Keychain Made with Pet Bottle Cap – TUTORIAL is not just a crochet pattern—it’s a creative way to recycle and make something fun, functional, and adorable. You’ve learned how to transform a simple bottle cap into a cute cactus with personality and purpose.
This project combines eco-conscious crafting, basic crochet skills, and personal touches to create something you’ll love to use or share. It’s fast, fun, and a great conversation starter.
If you enjoyed this tutorial, please leave your honest opinion in the comments and share any suggestions or customizations you tried. We’d love to see your creations and keep building a community of crafty recyclers and crochet enthusiasts!

From Duty-Free to Brand Stage
Airports are transforming from mere transit hubs to high-impact marketing platforms for alcohol brands. Once dominated by duty-free racks, terminals now serve as immersive brand stages offering travelers memorable experiences through interactive storytelling, digital experiences, and sensory brand engagements.
These experiential marketing strategies allow brands to communicate their identity in powerful ways—blending emotion, product, and setting. Duty-free shopping still boosts impulse purchases, but it's the experiential layer that builds customer loyalty.
Airports offer a unique blend of international foot traffic, affluent travelers, and high dwell time—making them ideal for target audience engagement through experience-driven marketing.
Why are airports important for alcohol marketing? They combine a captive, global audience with premium, customizable retail space and high dwell time.
How do airports help with storytelling? They enable brands to go beyond product display—offering interactive, multi-sensory engagements that connect emotionally with consumers.
The Strategic Power of Airports for Spirits Brands
Spirits brands are increasingly viewing airports as strategic marketing partners. Through pouring rights agreements, brands gain access to 90–95% market dominance within terminals, ensuring exposure to affluent and brand-loyal travelers.
Airports allow brands to align promotions with terminal-specific traits, seasonal trends, and passenger profiles. This highly customizable marketing strategy offers unmatched flexibility for brands seeking presence in travel retail.
Sponsorship funding, often built into these agreements, can be used to co-develop activations & sponsorships like wellness zones or sustainable initiatives—further deepening consumer connections.
What are pouring rights in airports? They grant a brand exclusive rights to sell in specific areas, ensuring market control and visibility.
How do spirits brands benefit? With tailored agreements and high-exposure zones, they maximize ROI, brand recognition, and sales.
High Dwell Time = More Time to Engage
Travelers spend extended periods post-security, which creates prime conditions for experiential events. This captive audience is more likely to browse, sample, and purchase—especially in premium alcohol categories.
Airport beverage activations like pop-ups, branded lounges, and sampling events harness this dwell time to build emotional connections and encourage impulse buying.
Placement of food and beverage airport outlets in key walkways boosts visibility, while the absence of time pressure enhances the potential for immersive experiences.
Why is dwell time important? Longer stays mean more brand exposure, interaction opportunities, and conversion potential.
Which formats work best? Pop-ups, modular activations, and lounges are ideal for using time-rich windows effectively.
Global Exposure with Premium Positioning
Airports are beverage brand powerhouses, combining global reach with premium placement. With digital billboards, geo-targeted mobile ads, and 3D media formats, brands can fine-tune messaging to real-time data.
This environment supports co-hosted custom events, product launches, and interactive experiences—elevating brand image and expanding social media reach through social media engagement.
Modern tools like trend tracking, social listening, and location-based analytics enhance strategy precision.
How do airports support premium positioning? Through curated experiences, exclusive zones, and data-driven display formats.
What’s the role of digital tech? It personalizes and localizes messaging, delivering the right campaign to the right traveler at the right time.
Frequent International Travelers: The Ideal Audience
Frequent flyers are brand-conscious, value-driven, and often shop for luxury alcohol products. Travel retail appeals to their tastes with limited-edition releases, personalized gifting, and co-branded collaborations.
Airport pop-ups provide a direct route to high-spending consumers, creating buzz-worthy brand moments that are both exclusive and engaging.
Why target frequent travelers? They have high buying power and are often seeking premium, exclusive experiences.
What kind of experiences resonate? Limited-time offers, VIP tastings, and culturally relevant product drops.
Timed Relevance: Gifting Seasons, Cultural Holidays, and “Last Chance” Moments
Peak travel aligns with holidays—creating perfect timing for experiential marketing campaigns centered around gifting. Branded wrapping, limited-time packaging, and “buy before you fly” incentives tap into the urgency and emotional nature of these purchases.
By customizing offerings to cultural moments, brands amplify emotional connection and purchasing intent.
What makes gifting moments powerful? They add emotional urgency, especially when paired with exclusivity and personalization.
What can brands do? Offer travel-exclusive SKUs, custom wrapping, and unique gifting add-ons.
Alcohol Brand Storytelling Strategies
Traditional advertising restrictions push alcohol brands toward experience-driven marketing. With a projected $1,594 billion market, competition fuels the need for cutting-edge storytelling.
Airport spaces provide the perfect setting for activations that blend story, space, and sampling, delivering on consumer expectations of authenticity and novelty.
Why are experiences replacing traditional ads? Because they’re memorable, shareable, and bypass restrictions.
What storytelling formats work? Branded lounges, cocktail classes, and interactive visual installations.
Traditional Signage and Its Impact
Despite the digital surge, traditional signage still plays a vital role. Static displays can reinforce digital campaigns and maintain brand presence where screens aren’t possible.
Airports integrate signage strategically to ensure maximum exposure during peak dwell times and along passenger paths.
Is signage still effective? Yes, especially when used with experiential and digital tactics.
Where is signage placed? Along high-traffic areas, boarding gates, and retail walkways.
Experiential Marketing in Airport Environments
Pop-ups and activations are more than just eye-catching—they’re a vehicle for deep consumer engagement. Travelers are in a positive, open mindset, which makes immersive brand experiences particularly impactful.
Examples include cocktail tastings, AR-enhanced displays, and collaborative lounges—all enhancing the passenger journey while boosting sales and loyalty.
Why is airport experiential marketing so effective? Because travelers are time-rich, relaxed, and open to new experiences.
Which formats work best? Sampling bars, concept lounges, and hybrid tech-infused installations.
Customizing Beverage Partnerships
No two airports are the same. Agreements are shaped by passenger demographics, space constraints, and strategic objectives. Flexibility in exclusivity levels allows beverage brands to balance visibility with local relevance.
Agreements may include eco-packaging commitments, wellness integrations, and dedicated program funding—increasing the impact of the partnership.
Can local brands be included? Yes, through custom carve-outs that support regional identity and engagement.
What makes these partnerships strategic? Their ability to align commercial, cultural, and sustainability goals.
Engaging Local and International Travelers
From signage to custom programs, airports craft unified passenger engagement strategies. Seasonal themes, dynamic pricing, and loyalty integrations let beverage brands tap into the global-local mix.
Aligning airport beverage options with real-time traveler preferences supports conversion and brand visibility.
How are local travelers engaged? Through themed activations, local SKUs, and collaborative campaigns with regional brands.
What about international passengers? Through globally recognized brand names, exclusive travel editions, and loyalty program integration.
Revenue Generation and Guest Experience
Pouring rights agreements generate long-term revenue while enhancing guest satisfaction. By controlling beverage offerings, airports can ensure consistency, quality, and variety—meeting traveler expectations.
Personalized experiences, modular activations, and co-branded lounges contribute to strong emotional bonds and repeat engagement.
How do alcohol partnerships generate revenue? Through exclusive deals, high-margin product sales, and marketing fees.
Do passengers benefit? Yes, through premium, personalized, and often exclusive retail experiences.
How Airports Benefit from Alcohol Partnerships
From sponsorship funding to brand-aligned activations, alcohol partnerships give airports new revenue streams and enhanced passenger amenities.
Flexible terms allow alignment with innovation strategy articles, wellness initiatives, or sustainability goals—tailored to each airport’s long-term vision.
How long do these partnerships last? Typically 10 years, but they can be adjusted based on need.
What else can they include? Eco-packaging, digital campaigns, and themed promotional events.
Tailoring Experiences to Diverse Tenant Needs
Flexibility is key. With a broad mix of tenants, airports require marketing agreements that adapt to individual goals—whether it's increasing spend, enriching experience, or promoting wellness.
By customizing activations & sponsorships, airports can cater to unique tenant profiles without sacrificing cohesion.
How is flexibility built in? Through modular terms, tailored media, and optional carve-outs.
Can smaller brands participate? Yes, via curated exceptions and thematic collaborations.
Enhancing Guest Satisfaction through Tailored Activations
Custom beverage experiences—sampling, mixology, lounges—elevate guest satisfaction. These memorable experiences turn passive travelers into active brand advocates.
Marketing tools, including digital signage, social media buzz, and geo-tagged promotions, reinforce brand impact and conversion.
What makes these activations effective? Personalization, interaction, and emotional resonance.
What’s the outcome? Stronger loyalty, increased sales, and extended brand reach.
Building Desire Through Exclusivity & Scarcity
Airport activations thrive on limited-time offers, exclusive SKUs, and scarcity-driven campaigns. These not only create urgency but also elevate perceived value.
Co-branded merchandise and limited drops add a tangible memory to the emotional engagement.
Why does scarcity work? It triggers urgency and increases perceived brand value.
What formats use this well? Airport-only bottle designs, personalized packaging, and limited-time discounts.
Loyalty Integrations with Frequent Flyer Programs
Alcohol brands can partner with loyalty programs to reward frequent flyers through exclusive products, co-branded campaigns, or custom lists of perks.
Digital tools track behavior, allowing for dynamic offers that align with passenger data and trip history—creating meaningful, tailored engagements.
Can loyalty programs include alcohol rewards? Yes, especially in travel retail formats with proper compliance.
How are these campaigns delivered? Via app push, in-terminal screens, and targeted digital promotions.
Case Studies in Effective Digital Alcohol Branding
From beer festivals to virtual events, the alcohol sector is evolving fast. Events like Craft Beer Rising in London show how immersive experiences elevate brand engagement.
1. Glenmorangie – “It’s Kind of Delicious and Wonderful” Airport Activation
Location: Madrid-Barajas, Heathrow, Paris CDG, Edinburgh, Istanbul
Overview: Moët Hennessy launched a multi-airport campaign for Glenmorangie featuring a playful barber shop-inspired activation. Designed to celebrate life's joyful rituals, the experience included facial massages and whisky tastings from Glenmorangie’s travel-exclusive range.
Why it matters: The brand successfully turned traditional duty-free space into a whimsical, experiential zone, aligning product messaging with comfort, indulgence, and discovery.
2. Aperol – Immersive Spritz Experience in European Airports
Location: Airports in Rome, Barcelona, Frankfurt
Overview: Campari Group rolled out a vibrant omnichannel campaign across major European hubs featuring branded lounges, Aperol Spritz sampling, exclusive merchandise, and social sharing incentives. The lounges were styled as “Living Room” spaces to reflect Aperol’s lifestyle positioning.
Why it matters: This activation positioned Aperol as a mood-defining brand for travelers heading into summer destinations, increasing relevance and memorability at the point of departure.
3. Jägermeister – “The Secret is You” U.S. Airport Campaign
Location: Over 60 U.S. airports including JFK, LAX, and O'Hare
Overview: Jägermeister launched a large-scale travel retail activation with interactive displays that celebrated individual expression. Designed to reflect its bold, youthful image, the campaign encouraged traveler participation through motion-driven screens and artistic visuals.
Why it matters: By aligning brand identity with audience self-expression, Jägermeister achieved both cultural relevance and interactive impact at scale.
4. The House of Suntory – Frankfurt Airport Boutique
Location: Frankfurt Airport, Germany
Overview: In partnership with Heinemann Duty Free, Suntory opened a premium whisky boutique focused on Japanese craftsmanship and heritage. The space offered education, seasonal narratives, and rare travel retail exclusives.
Why it matters: This activation blurred the line between retail and gallery space, elevating the perception of spirits in the airport environment and building long-term brand equity.
5. Buchanan’s – Premium Retail Display at Tocumen Airport
Location: Tocumen International Airport (a U.S.–LATAM–Europe transit hub)
Overview: Buchanan’s created a striking visual activation at Atenza Duty Free featuring illuminated displays, large-format branding, and custom furniture. The setup targeted affluent passengers en route to or from North America and Europe.
Why it matters: This case highlights how retail design and merchandising strategy alone can command traveler attention and build perceived value in a busy terminal environment.
How is success measured? Via footfall data, social shares, and sales uplift.
What makes digital branding effective? Precision targeting, interactivity, and cross-channel storytelling.
Designing Activations That Travel Well
Modular design, quick installs, and portable formats are essential for airport activations. They enable brands to execute at scale across diverse airport settings with minimal disruption.
Efficiency, adaptability, and impact are key—especially in environments with tight timelines and spatial limitations.
What are portable formats? Flexible, easy-to-install designs that adapt to different spaces.
Why do they matter? They reduce cost, speed up rollout, and enhance scalability.
Localized Storytelling: Matching the Airport to the City
From São Paulo to Paris, airports now mirror their host cities’ identity. Alcohol brands can tap into this by incorporating local narratives into activations—blending global presence with cultural resonance.
How does local storytelling help? It creates relevance, builds connection, and adds authenticity.
Examples? City-themed cocktails, locally sourced ingredients, or airport décor matching cultural festivals.
Results and Benefits of Airport Alcohol Branding
The result? Higher revenue, better experiences, and lasting impact. Whether it’s through loyalty integrations, interactive experiences, or sustainability-driven promotions, alcohol brands are setting new benchmarks in travel retail.
Is airport branding worth the investment? Yes—passenger engagement, visibility, and conversion rates are consistently strong.
What’s next for the industry? More personalization, smarter data use, and deeper experiential layers.
Increased Sales and Brand Visibility
Airports deliver a high-value combination of affluent consumers, exclusivity, and impulse-driven shopping moments. With 13% of passengers visiting airport bars and 84% planning to return within a year, the opportunity for retail activations and upselling is significant.
Unique environments like themed lounges, branded bars, and duty-free exclusives boost brand visibility and provide memorable shopping moments that differentiate airport retail from traditional outlets.
Why are airport sales stronger than traditional retail? Travelers are often in a celebratory or gift-buying mindset, increasing their likelihood of purchase.
How do brands stand out? Through high-visibility placements, exclusive products, and immersive branded environments.
Building Stronger Consumer Connections
Experiential marketing strategies build emotional bridges. At airports, brands engage with consumers through real-time activations—think tasting bars, AR packaging, or co-hosted lounge events. These interactive experiences foster loyalty by letting passengers associate travel with the brand.
When travelers photograph or share branded experiences online, the brand message extends far beyond the terminal—turning a single moment into long-tail engagement.
What kind of connections are created? Emotional, sensory, and memorable ones—resulting in higher brand recall and customer satisfaction.
Do people share these experiences? Yes. Branded lounges and eye-catching displays often go viral on social media platforms.
Long-term Brand Loyalty Development
Airport activations, when done right, create lasting brand affinity. The ability to deliver exclusive, immersive, and premium experiences not only supports one-time sales but nurtures deeper brand relationships over time.
By aligning with frequent flyer programs, eco-conscious values, and localized storytelling, alcohol brands can repeatedly connect with their ideal audience and build long-term loyalty.
Can loyalty be built in short interactions? Yes—if the experience is personalized, emotional, and exclusive.
How do airports help brands stay top-of-mind? Through consistent exposure, exclusive offerings, and integrated digital experiences.

NFRA to Implement Annual Audits, Aims to Strengthen Corporate Governance
The National Financial Reporting Authority (NFRA) is set to implement annual inspections of major audit firms, including the Big Four, to ensure compliance and deter deviations from established standards. The move aims to combat corporate fraud by aligning with global best practices and mirroring the US Public Company Accounting Oversight Board’s periodic inspections. NFRA’s decision follows December 2023 reports highlighting deficiencies in the audit processes of prominent firms like BSR & Co. and Deloitte. The regulator’s planned annual inspections, employing data analytics, will focus on auditors serving large public companies. While not intended as a regulatory crackdown, the inspections seek to enhance transparency and accountability in accounting practices. Identified “gross negligence” may prompt further investigation, ensuring prompt remedial actions and ongoing engagement between regulators and auditors on crucial issues.

Key Features of Baidu AI Answer
- Natural Language Processing (NLP):
Like Google’s AI capabilities, Baidu AI employs cutting-edge NLP to understand the intent behind user queries. This enables the system to interpret complex questions and provide accurate responses.
- Knowledge Graph Integration:
Leveraging a vast knowledge graph, Baidu delivers contextually relevant answers, ensuring precision. This approach is comparable to Google’s rich results but localised for the Chinese market.
- Real-Time Processing:
Baidu’s AI processes queries in real time, delivering up-to-date answers that reflect the latest available information. This ensures users access dynamic and relevant data without delays.
By catering to China’s unique linguistic and cultural context, Baidu AI positions itself as a leader in AI-driven search capabilities, offering a seamless and efficient user experience.
Baidu AI Agent: Intelligent Assistance for Businesses
Beyond search, Baidu has introduced the Baidu AI Agent, an AI-powered tool designed to enhance business operations through interactive and personalized support. More than just a chatbot, this platform allows businesses to train AI assistants with industry-specific knowledge and linguistic models, enabling customized interactions that align with their needs.
How Baidu AI Agent Supports Businesses
- Customisable AI Solutions:
The Baidu AI Agent’s customisation feature is particularly valuable for businesses operating in China or targeting Chinese-speaking customers. By training the AI Agent with industry-specific language models and business data, companies can ensure that the AI assistant understands and effectively addresses the intricacies of customer interactions in an engaging and contextually relevant manner.
- Enhanced User Experience:
The Baidu AI Agent offers users a more dynamic and responsive search experience. It can provide tailored recommendations, answer queries more precisely, and even anticipate user needs based on previous interactions. This level of personalisation significantly enhances user satisfaction and loyalty.
- Sales Optimisation:
Baidu AI Agents assist in recommending products, answering common questions, and guiding customers through purchasing journeys, leading to improved conversion rates and customer satisfaction.
The Future of AI in Search and Business
Baidu’s advancements in AI-driven search and business solutions reflect the growing role of artificial intelligence in China’s digital ecosystem. By improving search efficiency and enabling more personalized interactions, Baidu AI is shaping how users access information and how businesses connect with their customers. As AI technology continues to evolve, both individuals and enterprises can explore new ways to leverage these innovations for a smarter, more efficient digital experience.

Halo of Lightning (5e Spell)
3rd-level Evocation | |
Casting time: | 1 action |
Range: | Self |
Components: | V, S, M (a tiny drum) |
Duration: | Concentration, up to 10 minutes |
You create two lightning orbs that float behind your head for the duration. When you cast the spell and as an action on subsequent turns, you can make a ranged spell attack from each orb. These attacks have a range of 30ft and deal lightning damage equal to 1d12 plus your spellcasting modifier.
Alternatively, when you cast the spell or as an action on subsequent turns, you can launch any number of the orbs at targets within range. You can launch them at one target or several. If you choose to do so, make a spell attack roll for each orb. On a hit, an orb deals 3d10 lightning damage. Each orb you launch dissipates after hitting or missing.

Printed sticker labels are pretty expensive but they do look aesthetic to use on your jars or storage. Now, you don’t have to worry about how much sticker labels would cost you because this tutorial by Hometalk on YouTube will teach a DIY hack that only requires packing tape and ordinary printed texts. You can be creative and make other designs once you learned the basics.
- Printed label, ordinary
- Packing Tape
- Coin, key, or any small metal object
- Credit card or any hard card
- Bowl of water
Choose a font and design that you want, then print it out on regular paper, preferably a thick font style. Cut out the words then take a regular clear packing tape and place it on top of the words, use any small coin or key, and place it underneath one side of the tape so that it’s easier to remove. Take a credit card or any hard card and press it hard while rubbing it onto the tape.
Next, the most important step is to peel it off your table or flat surface then cut off the excess tape on the sides. Now, submerge it into a bowl of water, then let the label sit in the water for 1 minute to loosen the paper. Once the label is saturated, start rubbing off all the paper on the back until what’s left is just the text itself. Let the label dry completely and it will return to its stickiness. Attach the label to your jar or storage.

Stochastic Comparisons in Vacation Models.
Publication or External Link
We consider single-server queueing systems, known as vacation models, where at every service completion the server might either serve the next customer from the queue (if any) or take a vacation (i.e., become unavailable to the customers for a random period of time) depending on the service schedule of the model. Using coupling arguments, we make stochastic comparisons between quantities of interest of one vacation model to those of another vacation model under a different service schedule. We first establish a stochastic ordering for sequences of service completion epochs, from which stochastic comparisons for waiting time sequences and queue size processes can be deduced. These comparisons are then used to obtain some monotonicity results for vacation models with limited and Bernoulli service schedules.

EFFECT OF SECONDARY RADIOCLIMATIC VARIABLES ON SIGNAL PROPAGATION IN NSUKKA, NIGERIA
P.E. Okpani, P.A. Nwofe, and N.O. Chukwu
Department of Physics
This study presents the effects of Radioclimatic variables on signal propagation in Nsukka, Nigeria. The primary Radioclimatic data used in the study include temperature, pressure and humidity or water vapour pressure, while secondary radioclimatic data includes refractivity (N), refractive index (n) and effective earthâ€™s radius (k-factor). The measurements of the primary variables were made at time interval of 30 minutes daily from August 2013 to July 2014. The results obtained show seasonal variation of the temperature and relative humidity. This leads to the increase in the values of radio refractivity and refractive index. The results also, show that the k-factor values for those months were at the range 1.555 â€" 1.653. This high values of kfactor is an indicator to the possible causes of signal interference in the study area.
KEYWORDS: Refractivity, refractive index, k-factor, Nsukka.
It has been established that a good knowledge of secondary Radioclimatic data especially the surface refractivity as well as the diurnal and seasonal variability amongst other factors, are useful tools in planning terrestrial radio links mainly because of multi-path fading and interference effects. Tropospheric surface refractivity poses a major setback to the phenomenon of communication globally. Research done by Oyedun (2007), indicate that the interaction between some tropospheric factors and radio frequencies > 30 Mhz, exposes the signals to important propagation characteristics which often degrades communication links especially at higher frequencies. Korak, (2003) opined that the propagation of electromagnetic waves in the atmosphere (especially the troposphere) is greatly influenced by the composition of the atmosphere, and attributed it to the fluctuations of atmospheric parameters such as; temperature, pressure and relative humidity. Other important variable, â€�“the tropospheric refractive indexâ€Â� is also a function of pressure, temperature and humidity and this implies that fluctuations of these atmospheric parameters (pressure, temperature and humidity) do cause significant variation in the refractive index of the air in the troposphere. The consequences of this scenario lies in the fact that the signal propagating through the troposphere does not arrive at its destination with the same amount of energy with which it was propagated from the source. According to Hall (1980), the troposphere can be defined as that region which extends from the earth surface to an altitude of about 10km at the earth poles and 17km at the equator. Nsukka is located at latitude 6o 45N and longitude 7o 30E, South â€"Eastern Nigeria. The period of investigation spans across the two major seasons (wet and dry) in Nigeria. This paper aims to investigate the effects of Radioclimatic variables on signal propagation in Nsukka, with a view to establish potential pathways to improve radio/communication signal reception in the study area.

The course aims to teach a method of reading the built landscape through the knowledge of the forming processes common to urban fabrics and buildings. How to Choose a Monitoring Program for Asset-Based Lenders and Factors
UCC, Due Diligence, Reduce Financial Risk, Tips, Lien Monitoring, Events & TradeshowsIn honor of the 2014 Factoring Conference, we compiled some of our favorite tips on choosing a lien monitoring solution, a service that all asset-based lenders and factors need to help mitigate risk. Here is a quick ‘stress test’ you can give your lien monitoring approach to see if it’s up to scratch.
Does your lien monitoring program…
Quickly Alert You of Federal Tax Lien Filings?
Many creditors consider Federal Tax Liens the most urgent of all liens because they can prime a perfected UCC. For factors and asset-based lenders, it’s critical to know about a Notice of Federal Tax Lien as soon as possible, so they can act within the 45-day window—remember, a UCC only provides priority protection for 45 days if a Federal Tax Lien is filed.
“In the real world of getting tough deals done, nothing can replace the importance of knowing if and when a Federal Tax Lien is filed.”
– Darrel Pierce, Dkyema Law Group
Notify you of Junior UCC1 Filings and UCC3 Change Statements?
Monitoring debtor names for junior UCC1 filings can reveal if another creditor has taken a position with your debtor and the possible breaking of negative covenants. Monitoring for UCC3 change statements provides creditors timely information on any unauthorized changes to their perfected UCC, such as an unauthorized termination.
Include State Tax Liens and Judgment Liens?
Many secured parties also insist on monitoring for State Tax Liens and Judgment Liens. For State Tax Liens, being alerted right away if their borrower is failing to pay their taxes is vital as it could be an indication of financial distress, while Judgment liens can be a good indicator of character among other things.

Big Time Gaming – The Founding Fathers Of Modern Slot Mechanics
With its roots in Australia, Big Time Gaming (BTG) was founded by a team of industry veterans with the goal of shaking up the online slots scene. ]Players couldn’t get enough of the feeling of unpredictability and excitement that Megaways brought. The Megaways engine went on to power countless hits, both from BTG and through collaborations with other top studios, making it arguably the most influential innovation in modern slot history. BTG – Mechanics That Pack A Punch
While many studios rely on familiar formats or adopt mechanics like Megaways through licensing, Big Time Gaming stands out as the originator of some of the most influential features on online slot sites. Known for pushing boundaries and setting industry standards, BTG continues to craft in-house mechanics that blend high volatility with smart, player-focused design.
BTG’s signature mechanics appear across its portfolio and often serve as the blueprint for others to follow. Below, we’ve broken down some of the standout features you’ll find in BTG slots:
Megaways – Introduced in 2015 with Dragon Born, Megaways was a game-changer for online slots. This mechanic randomises the number of symbols per reel on each spin, allowing up to 117,649 ways to win. Instead of fixed paylines, Megaways slots feature between 2 and 7 symbols on each reel, creating a constantly shifting and dynamic experience. It wasn’t until the launch of Bonanza that the mechanic truly exploded in popularity, going on to be licensed by dozens of developers and becoming a defining feature in modern slot design.
Reel Adventure – Debuting in Holy Diver, this mechanic blends slot gameplay with progression-based elements. As players spin, they help a character journey through a level-based map, unlocking loot chests and bonus features along the way.
Win Exchange – As seen in games like Max Megaways, this mechanic gives players the option to trade moderate wins for a shot at triggering the bonus round. For example, if a player wins 100x or more, they can choose to enter the Free Spins directly instead of collecting the win.
Dual Reaction – Found in games like Extra Chilli, this mechanic is tied to cascading reels. After a win, the winning symbols disappear and new ones fall into place from above. This cascade includes the horizontal row of symbols above the middle reels. This increases the chance of chaining wins and hitting those all-important bonus symbols.
Extended Reels – Showcased in White Rabbit, this mechanic increases reel height during free spins. Japanese tuner Wald International has officially released their first two photos of the upcoming exterior kit for the 2013 Lexus LS. The Executive Line package features a front spoiler, revised skirt skirts, a rear bumper insert, a trunk-mounted spoiler, a reduced ride height and shiny alloy wheels.
The stock LS 460, 460 L and F SPORT are powered by a 4.6 liter V8 engine linked to an eight-speed sequential shift automatic transmission. The RWD model produces 386bhp with 487Nm of torque pushing the luxury saloon from zero to 60mph in 5.4 seconds before hitting an electronically limited top speed of 210km/h.
The AWD’s output is limited to 360bhp and 470Nm. In this case a sprint to 60mph is done in a slower 5.9 seconds. The LS 600h Hybrid is powered by 5.0 liter V8 engine and a pair of electric motors to produce a total output of 438bhp. This hybrid version does 0-60mph in 5.5 seconds.
The Lexus LS Executive Line package by Wald International will make its official debut at the Tokyo Auto Salon.
